original: World's oldest known cave painting of an animal - a pig, 45,000 years old, discovered in Leang Tedongnge cave, island of Sulawesi, Indonesia
Oldest material existing on earth at 7.5 billion years old revealed by scientists studying the Murchison meteorite that fell to earth in Australia in 1960s
"Mickey Mouse" comic strip 1st appears
NASA select its first American women astronauts
Galileo Galileidiscovers Callisto, 4th satellite of Jupiter
The National Geographic Society is founded in Washington, D.C. for "the increase and diffusion of geographic knowledge"
Henry Fordpatents a method of constructing plastic auto bodies
Two thirds of the Venus's southern hemisphere suddenly brightened as something triggered aerosols to form at a furious rate.
Philadelphia centerWilt Chamberlainscores 73 points in Warriors' 135-117 win over Chicago Packers; at the time, most points scored in NBA regulation game, and remains tied for 3rd-highest total
Playing in his 436th career game, Edmonton centerWayne Gretzkyscores his 400th career NHL goal and adds 2 assists in a 5-4 Oilers' win over the Sabres in Buffalo
Connor McDavid, Canadian ice hockey centre (#1 pick 2015 NHL Draft by Edmonton Oilers; Hart Memorial Trophy 2017, 21, 23; Art Ross Trophy 2017, 18, 21, 22, 23), born in Richmond Hill, Ontario

Lionel Messiscores for FC Barcelona in 3-0 win over Eibar, his 400th goal in La Liga; becomes first player to reach mark in just one of Europe's top 5 leagues; Cristiano Ronaldo had 409 in Spain, England & Italy
America3 becomes 1st all-female crew to win an America's Cup race
Rakesh Sharma, Indian cosmonaut (Soyuz T-11), first Indian in space, born in Patiala, India
99-year-old Otto Bucher from Switzerland becomes the oldest man to record a hole-in-one at Spanish GC La Manga's 130-yard 12th hole
American inventorRay Kurzweiland the National Federation of the Blind unveil the Kurzweil Reading Machine, the first omni-font optical character recognition system
Henri Farmanbecomes the first person to fly an observed circuit of more than 1km, winning the Grand Prix d'Aviation
Australian equals hottest temperature on record of 50.7C (123.26F) in Onslow, Western Australia
Arctic blast affects large parts of the US, with 44 million people under winter weather advisory; Chester, Montana registers continental US record low of 54 degrees below zero
Orlando Magic knock down a then NBA-record 23 3-pointers in 37 attempts (62.2%); Jameer Nelson goes 5-for-5 from beyond the arc as Magic beat the Sacramento Kings, 139-107
Early-morning ballistic missile alert sent across Hawaii in error, revoked after 38 minutes
World's largest religious festival, the Mahakumbh Mela for 400 million people begins beside the Ganges in Prayagraj, India. Held every 12 years, 2025 is the grand Kumbh Mela, held every 144 years.
Egan Bernal, Colombian road cyclist (Tour de France 2019; Giro d'Italia 2021), born in Bogotá, Colombia
Vitaly Scherbo, Belarusian gymnast (6 x Olympic gold CIS 1992; World C'ship gold x 12), born in Minsk, Belarus
Johnny Cashperforms for inmates at Folsom State Prison in Folsom, California; show is recorded for live album release
Chubby Checker's song "The Twist", credited with starting the Twist dance craze, goes to #1 in the charts two years after first reaching number one spot
Record Powerball lottery held in America - $1.6 billion, (3 winning tickets)

Houston guard James Harden scores 38 points in Rockets' 116-108 loss to Orlando; 16th straight game with 30+ points matching Kobe Bryant's NBA record streak; also goes 1 for 17 from 3-point arc, tying record for most misses
Swedish-American inventor E.F.W. Alexanderson, of General Electric, demonstrates the first television receiver at Proctors Theatre in Schenectady, New York
Bernice Gera wins anti-discrimination case against the National Baseball Congressl, initiated March 15, 1971 to be allowed to umpire
Andre Cason, American athlete (World C'ship gold 4 x 100m relay 1991 WR 37.50s, 1993; WR 60m indoor 6.41s 1992), born in Virginia Beach
Matthew Bourne, English chorographer, known for his version of "Swan Lake" with all-male swans, born in London
Cricket all-rounder Shakib Al Hasan scores Bangladesh's top Test score ever (217) during the 1st Test v New Zealand in Wellington
Christina von Saltza, American swimmer (Olympic gold 400m, 4x100m freestyle, 4x100m medley 1960), born in San Francisco, California
STS-54 (Endeavour) launches into orbit
HMS Tyger runs aground on coral reefs near Garden Key, Florida, (rediscovered 1993 and identified 2024)
-40°F (-40°C), Oakland, Maryland (state record)
Sergei Brylin, Russian ice hockey center (Stanley Cup 1995, 2000, 03 NJ Devils), born in Moscow, Russia
Nasa Hataoka, Japanese golfer (Women's PGA C'ship 2018 runner-up), born in Ibaraki, Japan

Dr. William Brydon, a surgeon in the British Army during the First Anglo-Afghan War, becomes famous for (reputedly) being the sole survivor of an army of 16,500 when he reaches the safety of a garrison in Jalalabad.
Earthquake measuring magnitude 7.6 strikes El Salvador, killing more than 840 people
Computers across Britain hit by "Friday the 13th"/Jerusalem virus
Hank AaronandFrank Robinsonelected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Robert C. Weaver, 1st Black person selected for cabinet by US PresidentLyndon B. Johnson(Housing and Urban Development - HUD)
Douglas Wilder, 1st elected African American Governor inaugurated (Virginia)
Super Bowl VIII, Rice Stadium, Houston, TX: Miami Dolphins beat Minnesota Vikings, 24-7; MVP: Larry Csonka, Miami, RB
South Africa cricket team's 1st win over Australia, at Adelaide
Sarah Caldwell is 1st woman to conduct at NYC Metropolitan Opera House as she led orchestra in a performance of "La Traviata"
The Church of England accepts the theory of evolution
Sabine Zlatin, Polish-born French nurse who tried save Izieu children from Nazis), born in Warsaw (d. 1996)
Sydney Brenner, South African biologist known for his contributions on the genetic code and molecular biology (Nobel Laureate 2002), born in Germiston, Transvaal, South Africa (d. 2019)
Shiv Kumar Sharma, Indian santoor (100-string dulcimer-like instrument) master, and composer, born in Jammu, British India (d. 2022)
Bill Bailey, British comedian (Black Books, QI), born in bath, England
Simon Shelton Barnes, British actor (Teletubbies-Tinky Winky), born in Bethnal Green, London (d. 2018)
Daevid Allen [Christopher David], Australian musician and performance artist (Soft Machine, Gong), born in Melbourne, Victoria (d. 2015)
Euzhan Palcy, Martinican film director (A Dry White Season, Sugar Cane Alley) and the first Black woman to direct a film for a major Hollywood studio, born in Martinique, French West Indies
Alan Taylor, American television and film director (The Sopranos; Game of Thrones; Thor; The Many Saints of Newark), born in San Francisco, California
Suggs [Graham McPherson], English singer-songwriter (Madness), born in Hastings, England
Wayne Coyne, American singer-songwriter, guitarist, keyboardist, and theremin player (The Flaming Lips - "Do You Realize??"), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
(Clarence) "Bud" Anderson, US Army Air Force flying ace (WWII), US Air Force officer (Vietnam), and test pilot, born in Oakland, California (d. 2024)[1]
